rationale.policy: Each limit is a hard raw-byte/entry ceiling enforced by viteBudgetPlugin during the production build (Rollup generateBundle) and the Workbox precache manifest transform. One byte or one entry over any limit fails the build. Baselines are the current measured actuals plus a small (~4-5%) headroom so routine edits do not flap CI while a material regression is caught.
rationale.initialEntryBytes: Raw bytes of the synchronously-loaded entry chunk (index-*.js). The web-architecture plan's original target was 423707, baselined against a smaller plan-time entry; tasks 1-9 grew the eager entry (pure state cores + consolidated grammar scanner are imported at startup) to ~440910 raw (measured 2026-07-16 in generateBundle), ABOVE that 423707 target. Every other budget here matches the plan number exactly (each is actual + ~4-5% headroom); 423707 alone is stale because the real entry outgrew it, so enforcing it literally would make the build permanently red rather than catch regressions. Rebaselined to the current actual + ~4.8% headroom, consistent with the headroom methodology of the other five budgets. See task-10-report.md for the full discrepancy write-up; the 462016 rebaseline was ratified by the user on 2026-07-16 as the permanent limit (no entry-shrink follow-up owed). Re-measured for the PDF viewer feature (pkm-srek, 2026-07-16): actual is 445507, ~1.04% above the ~440910 reference above but still comfortably under this 462016 limit; the growth traces to PdfEmbed.tsx's fallback-link wrapper logic and the new PdfFallbackLink.tsx component, both of which must render eagerly (before the lazy viewer chunk loads) — PdfViewer.tsx itself (which pulls in react-pdf/pdfjs-dist) is only reached via the dynamic `import("./PdfViewer")` in PdfEmbed.tsx, so no PDF-library code leaked into the eager entry. Limit intentionally left unchanged.
rationale.largestAssetBytes: Largest single output file. Now set by the pdf.js worker asset (app-assets/pdf.worker.min-*.mjs), not the sqlite3 wasm binary that used to hold this record (~864752): the worker is an emitted .mjs asset outside Rollup's chunk graph and measures ~1046214 raw (measured 2026-07-16, pkm-srek PDF viewer). Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (1093294) for a minor pdf.js version bump without silently precaching a much larger binary.
rationale.totalOutputBytes: Sum of every emitted output file. Grew to 6782567 raw (measured 2026-07-16) from the pre-PDF-viewer 5520272 limit because the PDF viewer family (react-pdf + pdfjs-dist lazy chunk, plus the pdf.js worker asset) joined the build. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7087783). Guards against overall bundle bloat regardless of chunk split.
rationale.precacheBytes: Sum of the Workbox precache manifest entry sizes. This is the offline app-shell weight a cold install downloads. Grew to 6790858 raw (measured 2026-07-16) from the pre-PDF-viewer 5494604 limit once the pdf.js worker asset and the PDF viewer chunk joined the precache manifest (the viewer must work offline). Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (7096447). Must stay bounded so install stays fast and quota-safe.
rationale.precacheEntries: Number of precache manifest entries. Measured 76 (2026-07-16) with the PDF viewer family in the manifest; rebaselined to actual + 3 (79), tighter than the prior 82 limit, since the real entry count did not grow as much as the PDF worker+chunk addition alone would suggest (offset by unrelated upstream chunk consolidation). Guards against an explosion of precached files (e.g. a new eagerly-split dependency family).
rationale.mermaidOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the Mermaid module graph (Rollup module ownership, not output file-name substrings). Mermaid stays fully precached / never-online under this explicit cap; ownership is all-or-nothing per chunk so the cap cannot absorb unrelated code. Re-measured for the PDF viewer feature (pkm-srek, 2026-07-16): actual is 3429238, still under this 3461961 limit (unchanged) though with less headroom (~0.94%) than the nominal ~4.5% convention, from unrelated drift accumulated since this cap was set rather than anything in the PDF viewer commits (no mermaid-related files changed on this branch). Limit intentionally left unchanged; worth revisiting if it goes over budget in a future unrelated change.
rationale.pdfjsOwnedBytes: Raw bytes of chunks WHOLLY owned by the lazy PDF viewer module graph (PdfViewer.tsx + react-pdf + pdfjs-dist; Rollup module ownership, not file-name substrings), mirroring mermaidOwnedBytes. Measured 2026-07-16 at 465880 raw (the PdfViewer-*.js chunk), confirming the ownership seeds correctly recognize the viewer chunk as wholly owned now that it exists; this replaces the deliberately-failing placeholder of 1 used while the chunk did not exist yet. Rebaselined to actual + ~4.5% headroom (486845). The pdf.js worker is an emitted .mjs asset rather than a chunk, so it is bounded by largestAssetBytes/totalOutputBytes/precacheBytes instead of this cap.
